scope.name: MERGE_PROOF
scope.purpose: Define the canonical merge-proof object that binds the actual merge commit to the full validated merge-legitimacy chain. Merge success is not proof. A governed merge requires a deterministic proof object bound to the exact validated_head_sha, resulting merge_commit_sha, PREO_VALID hash, SCO_VALID hash when required, approval lineage hash, and branch-protection enforcement hash. MERGE_PROOF_VALID is recorded finality evidence, not merge authorization.

merge_proof_object.sco_valid_hash_note: sco_valid_hash must be present and bound to the validated_head_sha when governed files are present in the merged PR diff. It must be explicitly null and recorded when SCO governance is not required — it must never be absent.

preo_valid_head_sha_match.rule: The preo_valid_hash in the proof must correspond to a PREO_VALID object whose head_sha equals the merged PR validated_head_sha. Stale or displaced PREO_VALID evidence is not valid input.

sco_valid_when_required.rule: When governed files are present in the merged PR diff, sco_valid_hash must be present and must correspond to a SCO_VALID object covering those files for the validated_head_sha.

approval_lineage_head_sha_match.rule: The approval_lineage_hash must correspond to an APPROVAL_LINEAGE_VALID object bound to the same validated_head_sha as the merged PR. Stale approvals are not valid inputs.

branch_protection_enforcement_head_sha_m.rule: The branch_protection_enforcement_hash must correspond to enforcement evidence evaluated for the same validated_head_sha.

merge_commit_sha_present.rule: The merge_commit_sha produced by the merge event must be present in the proof. Absence of merge_commit_sha means no proof can be generated.

validated_lineage_equals_merged_lineage.rule: The validated merge lineage (PREO_VALID + SCO_VALID + APPROVAL_LINEAGE + branch protection) must correspond to the lineage of the actual merge commit. Any divergence is a lineage mismatch.

trust_anchor.merge_actor_is_evidence: merged_by is event actor evidence included in the proof but is not the trust anchor.
trust_anchor.validator_is_attestation: The Governance Validator process is the canonical signer. validator_signature is the proof finality attestation.
trust_anchor.rule: The merge actor must be recorded in the proof. The merge actor must not be the trust anchor. The validator_signature over the canonical_proof_hash is the trust anchor.

missing_preo_valid_hash.condition: preo_valid_hash is absent, null, or empty.

missing_sco_valid_hash.condition: sco_valid_hash is absent when governed files are present in the merged PR diff.

missing_approval_lineage_hash.condition: approval_lineage_hash is absent, null, or empty.

missing_branch_protection_enforcement_ha.condition: branch_protection_enforcement_hash is absent, null, or empty.

missing_merge_commit_sha.condition: merge_commit_sha is absent, null, or empty.

merge_commit_not_bound_to_validated_head.condition: The merge_commit_sha is not a descendant of the validated_head_sha, or the gate hashes were not produced for the same validated_head_sha as the merged PR.

stale_validated_head_sha.condition: The validated_head_sha in the proof does not match the head_sha of the merged PR at the time of merge. Any gate hash produced for a different head_sha is stale evidence.

missing_validator_signature.condition: The validator_signature attesting proof finality is absent, null, or empty.

proof_hash_mismatch.condition: The canonical_proof_hash recomputed from proof fields does not match the stored canonical_proof_hash, or the validator_signature does not verify against the canonical_proof_hash and validator_key_id.

missing_merge_actor.condition: merged_by is absent, null, or empty.

missing_merge_timestamp.condition: merged_at is absent, null, or empty.

missing_validator_key_id.condition: The validator_key_id identifying the canonical signer is absent, null, or empty.

canonical_compression.rule_2: A governed merge requires proof bound to: exact validated_head_sha, resulting merge_commit_sha, preo_valid_hash, sco_valid_hash when required, approval_lineage_hash, and branch_protection_enforcement_hash.
canonical_compression.rule_3: No merge proof → no merge legitimacy.
canonical_compression.rule_4: Actor is evidence. Validator is attestation. Lineage is legitimacy.
canonical_compression.rule_5: MERGE_PROOF_VALID is recorded finality evidence, not merge authorization.

deterministic_artifact.rule: Identical gate hash inputs plus identical merge event fields must produce an identical canonical_proof_hash. The proof must be reproducible from its constituent fields alone.
